mission: Rork dependency removal / IVX ownership cutover — phased, no-crash
rule: Do not crash app. Phased cutover with rollback. Do not remove working routes before IVX replacement exists.

phase1_freeze.rollbackTagNote: Tags cannot be created from the Rork-managed sandbox (no push access to origin). The stable SHA d0d7f91 is recorded as the rollback point. On the independent GitHub checkout, run: git tag pre-rork-removal-stable d0d7f91 && git push origin pre-rork-removal-stable

rorkDependenciesFound.reason: Rork cloud bundler/preview REQUIRES this SDK; auto-restores it if removed; removing in-sandbox breaks live preview and achieves nothing. Must be removed on the independent GitHub/Render checkout via expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs.
Same as expo-sdk — paired with the SDK; removed together by the cutover script on the independent checkout.
Rork sandbox uses this to know app paths. Removing it in-sandbox breaks the workspace. Deleted by the cutover script on the independent checkout.

rorkDependenciesFound.item: RORK_TOOLKIT_NATIVE_URL, isRorkToolkitBaseUrl, getRorkToolkitKey, callRorkToolkitNative, rork toolkit candidate in getGatewayBaseUrlCandidates
rorkDependenciesFound.runtimeImpact: active AI fallback — backend could route AI traffic to https://toolkit.rork.com/text/llm/

rorkDependenciesFound.item: rork_toolkit in IVXProviderName type, callRorkToolkit, hasRorkToolkitKey, getRorkToolkitKey, rork_toolkit chain link in attemptProviderFallback
rorkDependenciesFound.runtimeImpact: active AI fallback — 3rd-tier fallback to https://toolkit.rork.com/text/llm/
EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY was consumed by backend getRorkToolkitKey() as an AI key fallback. The other 4 are Rork sandbox/preview plumbing.
rorkDependenciesFound.reason: These are injected by the Rork platform into the sandbox. Must be deleted from Render/Expo dashboards on the independent checkout (the cutover script reports them; it never edits secrets).

rorkDependenciesFound.item: EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_API_BASE_URL, EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_AUTH_URL, EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_FUNCTIONS_URL, EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY, EXPO_PUBLIC_TOOLKIT_URL (empty string literals)
rorkDependenciesFound.runtimeImpact: zero — empty literals, never read by Swift code at runtime
rorkDependenciesFound.status: REMOVED (file is auto-generated and will reset on next iOS build; the empty literals had zero runtime impact)

phase3_replaceRorkToolkit.status: Backend AI runtime replaced. Expo SDK/metro/env: NOT replaceable in-sandbox (see phase2). Must run cutover script on independent checkout.
ivxReplacementsInBackendCode: Primary: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY → https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v3/ai (Vercel AI Gateway, IVX-owned key)

phase4_aiProviderCutover.testsRun: 25 tests passed across ivx-blocker-fix.test.ts, public-chat-ai.test.ts, services/ivx-rork-independence.test.ts (0 failures)
phase4_aiProviderCutover.blocker: On the deployed Render backend, AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is currently set to a rork_sk_* key (per prior proof 2026-07-06). After this code change deploys, that key will NO LONGER BE ACCEPTED. You MUST set AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY to a real Vercel AI Gateway key (or set OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) on Render BEFORE deploying this cutover, or the AI chat room will throw 'IVX AI runtime is not configured.' This is the no-crash rule.

phase5_expoAndroidCutover.reason: The Rork cloud preview/bundler requires @rork-ai/toolkit-sdk + withRorkMetro. Removing them in-sandbox breaks the live preview and Rork auto-restores them (documented in expo/scripts/verify-expo-sdk.mjs). The prepared cutover script expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs handles this on the independent GitHub/Render checkout.
phase5_expoAndroidCutover.runOnIndependentCheckout: IVX_ALLOW_RORK_CUTOVER=1 node expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs && cd expo && bun install && bunx expo start

phase6_iosCutover.rorkSdk: none — iOS uses native Swift, no npm dependency
phase6_iosCutover.rorkUrlsInSwift: none at runtime (Config.swift empty literals removed; they were never read)
phase6_iosCutover.rorkEnvDependencyAtRuntime: none — all AI flows go through https://api.ivxholding.com
phase6_iosCutover.directIvxApiCalls: confirmed — OwnerAuthService, all views call api.ivxholding.com directly

phase7_backendCutover.rorkProofHelpersNote: verification-proof/*.json files are historical audit records. They reference 'rork' in filenames/content because they document past state. They are NOT runtime dependencies and removing them would destroy audit history. Left intact.

phase8_proofDeploySystem.status: GitHub/Render/Supabase verification already IVX-owned (GITHUB_TOKEN, RENDER_API_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY are IVX credentials, not Rork)
phase8_proofDeploySystem.rorkProofHelpers: none — proof scripts use IVX-owned credentials

phase9_deploySafely.status: NOT DEPLOYED — blocked on env key rotation (no-crash rule)
phase9_deploySafely.reason: Deploying the backend cutover BEFORE rotating AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY away from the rork_sk_* value will crash the AI chat room. The code change is committed locally but MUST NOT be deployed until a real Vercel AI Gateway key (or OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) is set on Render.
phase9_deploySafely.typecheck: pre-existing tsc env errors (missing bun/node type defs) unrelated to cutover; no new type errors introduced
deploySequence: 1. On Render dashboard: set AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY to a real Vercel AI Gateway key (or set OPENAI_API_KEY and/or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as direct fallbacks)
2. Remove EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY and RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY from Render env (no longer accepted)
3. Push the cutover commit to GitHub main → Render auto-deploys
6. Verify POST /api/ivx/owner-ai with owner credentials → real AI answer
7. If any step fails: git revert to d0d7f91 and redeploy

phase10_finalVerification.STATE: PHASE 4 (backend AI cutover) COMPLETE IN CODE. PHASES 5+9 BLOCKED ON INDEPENDENT CHECKOUT + ENV ROTATION. NO-CRASH RULE ENFORCED.
phase10_finalVerification.IVX_REPLACEMENTS: Vercel AI Gateway (primary) + OpenAI direct + Anthropic direct (fallbacks)
phase10_finalVerification.ANDROID_STATUS: blocked — Expo SDK/metro removal must run on independent checkout via cutover script (in-sandbox removal breaks preview + auto-reverts)
phase10_finalVerification.IOS_STATUS: rork-free at runtime — no action needed (Config.swift empty stubs were zero-impact)
phase10_finalVerification.WEB_STATUS: n/a — landing page consumes api.ivxholding.com, no Rork dependency
phase10_finalVerification.BACKEND_STATUS: code cutover complete, 25 tests pass, NOT YET DEPLOYED (env rotation gate)
phase10_finalVerification.AI_PROVIDER_STATUS: Rork toolkit removed from primary + fallback chain. BLOCKER: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY on Render is currently a rork_sk_* key — MUST be rotated to a real Vercel/OpenAI/Anthropic key BEFORE deploying or AI chat crashes
phase10_finalVerification.DEPLOY_STATUS: blocked on env key rotation (no-crash rule)
phase10_finalVerification.ROLLBACK_TAG: pre-rork-removal-stable NOT YET CREATED — run on independent checkout: git tag pre-rork-removal-stable d0d7f91 && git push origin pre-rork-removal-stable
BLOCKERS: BLOCKER 1 (deploy gate): AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY on Render is set to a rork_sk_* key. After the cutover code deploys, this key is rejected. Rotate to a real Vercel AI Gateway key OR set OPENAI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY BEFORE deploying, or the AI chat room crashes. This is the no-crash rule.

BLOCKER 2 (Expo SDK): @rork-ai/toolkit-sdk + withRorkMetro cannot be removed from the Rork sandbox — the cloud bundler requires them and auto-restores them. Run expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs on the independent GitHub/Render checkout (IVX_ALLOW_RORK_CUTOVER=1).
BLOCKER 3 (rollback tag): git tags cannot be pushed from the Rork sandbox. Create pre-rork-removal-stable on the independent checkout.
BLOCKER 4 (env vars): EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_* and RORK_TOOLKIT_SECRET_KEY must be deleted from Render/Expo dashboards on the independent checkout (the cutover script reports them; it never edits secrets).
phase10_finalVerification.FINAL_STATUS: PARTIAL — backend AI cutover complete in code (Rork toolkit fully removed from runtime + fallback chain, 25 tests pass). Full cutover requires: (1) env key rotation on Render, (2) running the cutover script on the independent GitHub checkout, (3) deploy + verify. NOT VERIFIED as complete until all 4 blockers are resolved on the independent checkout.
